annotation.text:
This example stream collects AI-backed usage events from the fictional company Forma's design-and-marketing SaaS.

It cleans invalid and duplicate data, enriches it with customer and outcome-based information from external systems, and assigns credit weights for use in Pricing. Invalid events are sent to Data Correction to prevent revenue leakage.

It also calculates a unit cost per AI usage event and aggregates those per customer and month, to track AI spending per customer for use in margin analytics.

All external integrations are simulated in this example stream so you can run it out of the box, but the flow and logic are similar to real-world use cases.
Send the clean, outcome-based, published-only, credith-weighted events for Pricing.
Send AI cost per customer data to an external BI dashboard showing revenue, cost and margin per customer and totals.

Invalid events go to Data Correction where they can be repaired and reprocessed. 

Duplicate events are discarded.

Normalize events into a standard format, and enrich events with customer ids.

Validate that a user belongs to a valid customer. If not, events go to Data Correction where they can be repaired and reprocessed.
Three difference source systems, three different data formats.
Calculate AI vendor cost per customer based on per-event unit cost, for both publish and non-publish usage events.

Successful outcomes = published assets.

Assign credit cost per event based on the service used and asset type.
